mechanism_narrative: Rising real personal consumption expenditures, as proxied by retail sales (RSAFS), indicate strengthening consumer demand that drives corporate earnings higher, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending accounts for ~70% of US GDP
failure_condition: Rank IC of RSAFS level against forward 20-day returns remains below 0.02 over a 5-year rolling window; or a long-only strategy triggered by above-trend RSAFS produces a Sharpe ratio < 0.5.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained drop in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 7.5% signals a tightening labor market that boosts consumer confidence and spending, supporting equity prices over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls below 7.5% on a monthly release and stays below for at least one additional month
evidence_refs: fact:Unemployment rate was 7.8% as of 2012-09-01 vintage
failure_condition: After the trigger activates, the average 60-day forward return is not statistically different from the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.10); or the hit rate of positive returns is less than 55%.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth (GDPC1) is weak but the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is near zero, the joint signal indicates aggressive monetary accommodation that boosts equity valuations through lower discount rates and a rising risk appetite.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 vintage 2012Q1 was $13,548.5B, well below potential
failure_condition: The interaction term (GDPC1 growth * FEDFUNDS level) in a regression of 20-day returns has a coefficient not statistically significant at the 5% level over any 3-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: In a near-zero interest rate environment (FEDFUNDS ≤ 0.25%), the commitment to accommodative policy lowers the equity risk premium and supports valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FOMC has held rates near zero since December 2008
failure_condition: Mean 20-day return during ZLB episodes is less than the mean return in non-ZLB periods with a t-statistic below 2.0; or the cumulative return under the strategy is negative over a 1-year walk-forward.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in industrial production (INDPRO) above the 98 index level signals a manufacturing recovery that translates into higher corporate profits and positive equity returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO exceeds 98 for two consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO vintage August 2012 was 96.82, still below 100
failure_condition: After trigger, forward 20-day return is negative in more than 50% of cases; or the average annualized return difference between trigger-on and trigger-off periods is less than 2%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous robust growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates a self-reinforcing economic expansion where rising employment boosts housing demand, supporting broad equity market gains over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: Monthly change in PAYEMS > 150,000 AND HOUST > 800,000 units
evidence_refs: fact:Historical correlation between payrolls and housing starts is high during recoveries
failure_condition: When both conditions are met, the average 60-day forward return is not significantly different from zero (t-test p > 0.05); or the proportion of positive returns is less than 60%.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained multi-month pickup in housing starts (HOUST) signals a recovery in residential investment that generates positive sentiment and short-term momentum in equities over the following week.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises for three consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:Housing starts were 750K in Aug 2012, recovering from trough of ~478K in 2009
failure_condition: After a three-month rising streak in HOUST, the next 5-day S&P 500 return is positive less than 55% of the time; or the average return is less than 50 basis points.

mechanism_narrative: A low and stable CPI inflation rate (CPIAUCSL) confirms that the Fed's accommodative stance is not yet threatened by price pressures, allowing low real rates to persist and sustain equity valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:CPI year-over-year growth was around 1.7% in mid-2012, below the Fed's 2% target
failure_condition: The correlation between CPIAUCSL level and 20-day forward returns is positive and statistically significant (p < 0.05) in a walk-forward test, contradicting the hypothesis; or the trading strategy based on below-target CPI produces a negative Sharpe ratio over 3 years.
